mapper.select: select a.* from sys_depart a join sys_user_depart b on a.id = b.dep_id where b.user_id = #{userId} ORDER BY org_type desc

SELECT t1.depart_name AS lev1, t1.id lev1Id, t2.depart_name AS lev2, t2.id lev2Id, t3.depart_name AS lev3, t3.id lev3Id, t4.depart_name AS lev4, t4.id lev4Id, t5.depart_name AS lev5, t5.id lev5Id, c.org_type orgType, c.id orgId FROM sys_depart AS t1 LEFT JOIN sys_depart AS t2 ON t2.parent_id = t1.id LEFT JOIN sys_depart AS t3 ON t3.parent_id = t2.id LEFT JOIN sys_depart AS t4 ON t4.parent_id = t3.id LEFT JOIN sys_depart AS t5 ON t5.parent_id = t4.id, sys_user_depart AS b, sys_depart AS c WHERE b.user_id = #{userId} AND b.dep_id = c.id AND CASE c.org_type WHEN '1' THEN t1.id = b.dep_id WHEN '2' THEN t2.id = b.dep_id WHEN '3' THEN t3.id = b.dep_id WHEN '4' THEN t4.id = b.dep_id WHEN '5' THEN t5.id = b.dep_id ELSE 0 = 1 END limit 1
select id from sys_depart where parent_id=#{parentId}
